mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a robust labor market and strong aggregate demand, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The walk-forward rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 20-day forward returns is consistently below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, highly accommodative monetary policy lowers the discount rate for future earnings and encourages risk-taking, boosting equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% is not statistically greater than the average return when FEDFUNDS >= 0.25% (t-test p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both retail sales and housing starts signals a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand, leading to lower forward earnings estimates and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 0 AND 3-month change in HOUST < 0
failure_condition: When both series show negative 3-month changes, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate from a low base indicates a potential negative turning point in the business cycle, signaling an imminent recession and causing a negative repricing of equity risk.
failure_condition: Following a trigger event, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 45% of the time over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects a healthy supply side of the economy, translating into higher corporate revenues and profitability, thus driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between INDPRO 12-month changes and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is not statistically positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the policy rate remains low, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This increases uncertainty about the magnitude of future monetary tightening and compresses real earnings, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods matching the trigger condition, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative and statistically significant (one-sided t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP is a direct signal of economic recession, causing investors to revise earnings expectations downward and de-risk portfolios, resulting in negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: In the quarter following a negative real GDP print, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is already tight (low unemployment), a surprisingly strong payrolls report increases expectations for imminent monetary policy tightening, causing a negative short-term reaction in equities as discount rates rise.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with active monetary policy
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero or is positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
